title: Verify I2C addressing and ACK handshake first
description: Confirm the seven-bit device address and per-byte acknowledgement on a capture before debugging any higher-level register logic.
body: Use when an I2C peripheral does not respond as expected. Do not assume the address from documentation alone, since many parts shift the address by the read/write bit in their datasheet. Actions: capture the bus, inspect the start condition and address byte, and verify the slave drives ACK low after the address and each data byte. Validation signals include a logic-analyzer trace showing ACK after the address and clean stop conditions. Risk boundary: writing to a wrong address can corrupt an unrelated device's registers, so deny blind register writes until the address ACK is confirmed.
